title: Valve Authority for HVAC Coil Applications
summary: Valve authority in the range of 30 to 50% is adequate for HVAC coil applications with 2:1 branch-to-riser pressure drop ratios. Higher Δt coils require less valve authority. Authorities less than 30% should be avoided.
evidence_quote: Typically, authorities in the range of 30 to 50% (for piping systems with 2:1 branch-to-riser pressure drop ratios) are adequate for HVAC coil applications, with higher Δt coils requiring less valve authority. Authorities less than 30% should be avoided

title: Control Valve Sizing Basis
summary: Control valves should be sized based on the required valve coefficient Cv for the required pressure drop and flow.
evidence_quote: Control valves should be sized on the basis of the required valve coefficient Cv for the required pressure drop and flow.

title: Coil Rating Parameters for Dehumidifying Coils
summary: Dehumidifying coils are rated within parameters including entering air dry-bulb 18 to 38°C, air face velocity 1 to 4 m/s, and entering chilled-water temperature 1.5 to 18°C.
evidence_quote:
Entering air dry-bulb temperature: 18 to 38°C
Air face velocity: 1 to 4 m/s
Entering chilled-water temperature: 1.5 to 18°C

title: Dehumidifying Coil Air Face Velocity Selection Range
summary: Dehumidifying coils for comfort applications are frequently selected in the range of 2.0 to 2.5 m/s air face velocity.
evidence_quote: Dehumidifying coils for comfort applications are frequently selected in the range of 2.0 to 2.5 m/s air face velocity.

title: Low-Temperature Heating System Supply Water Temperatures
summary: Low-temperature heating systems are generally designed with supply water temperatures from 80 to 115°C and temperature drops from 10 to 55 K.
evidence_quote: They are generally designed with supply water temperatures from 80 to 115°C and temperature drops from 10 to 55 K.

title: Valve Authority for Three-Way Valve Circuit
summary: For a three-way valve, the pressure drop in the bypass balancing valve should be set to equal that in the coil (load).
evidence_quote: The pressure drop in the bypass balancing valve in the three-way valve circuit should be set to equal that in the coil (load).

title: Coil Rating Entering Air Wet-Bulb Temperature
summary: Entering air wet-bulb temperature for coil ratings is 15 to 30°C if air is not dehumidified in the application.
evidence_quote: Entering air wet-bulb temperature: 15 to 30°C (if air is not dehumidified in the application, select coils based on sensible heat transfer)

title: Coil Rating Water Velocity Range
summary: Water velocity for coil ratings is 0.3 to 2.4 m/s. For ethylene glycol solution, it is 0.3 to 1.8 m/s.
evidence_quote:
Water velocity: 0.3 to 2.4 m/s
For ethylene glycol solution: 0.3 to 1.8 m/s

title: Dehumidifying Coil Sensible Heat Ratio Range
summary: The air-side ratio of sensible to total heat removed by dehumidifying coils varies from about 0.6 to 1.0 (sensible heat is 60 to 100% of total).
evidence_quote: The air-side ratio of sensible to total heat removed by dehumidifying coils varies in practice from about 0.6 to 1.0 (i.e., sensible heat is from 60 to 100% of the total, depending on the application).

title: Standard Rating Point for Unitary Equipment
summary: A standard rating point for unitary equipment is 54 L/s per kilowatt of refrigeration effect, not to exceed 60 L/s per kilowatt.
evidence_quote: For example, a standard rating point has been 54 L/s per kilowatt of refrigeration effect, not to exceed 60 L/s per kilowatt for unitary equipment.

title: Refrigerant Superheat for Standard Rating
summary: Refrigerant duty for standard rating would be 3 to 6 K superheat for an appropriate balance at 7°C saturated suction.
evidence_quote: Refrigerant (e.g., R-22) duty would be 3 to 6 K superheat for an appropriate balance at 7°C saturated suction.

title: Water Coil Circuitry Operating Parameters
summary: For water coils, circuitry would operate at 1.2 m/s, 5.5°C inlet water, 6.7 K rise (or 36 mL/s per kilowatt of refrigeration effect).
evidence_quote: For water coils, circuitry would operate at 1.2 m/s, 5.5°C inlet water, 6.7 K rise (or 36 mL/s per kilowatt of refrigeration effect).

title: Standard Rating Entering Air Conditions
summary: Standard ratings at 26.7°C db and 19.5°C wb are representative of entering air conditions for many comfort operations.
evidence_quote: The standard ratings at 26.7°C db and 19.5°C wb are representative of the entering air conditions encountered in many comfort operations.

title: Airflow Resistance Reduction with Hydrophilic Coils
summary: Tests have shown as much as a 30% reduction in air pressure drop across a hydrophilic coil as opposed to a new untreated coil.
evidence_quote: Tests have shown as much as a 30% reduction in air pressure drop across a hydrophilic coil as opposed a new untreated coil.

title: Freezing Prevention in Dehumidifying Coils
summary: Freezing does not occur with standard coils for comfort installations unless the refrigerant evaporating temperature at the coil outlet is below –4 to –2°C saturated.
evidence_quote: Freezing does not occur with standard coils for comfort installations unless the refrigerant evaporating temperature at the coil outlet is below –4 to –2°C saturated; the exact value depends on the design of the coil, its operating dew point, and the amount of loading.
